notes.content: The Rausser College of Natural Resources (RCNR) admits students directly into one of its nine undergraduate majors as selected by the applicant at the time of application. Because Rausser College does not admit transfer students as undeclared, each individual major has a distinct set of transfer courses required as major preparation before the applicant is minimally eligible for admission. Resources for RCNR transfer applicants: UC Berkeley Office of Undergraduate Admissions Minimum Requirements for Transfer Admission Rausser College of Natural Resources Transfer Applicants page - includes transfer admission guidelines specific to each Rausser College major, as well as additional information on how to submit a competitive application for your intended major Rausser College Major Requirements and Sample Degree Plans Rausser College Advising Staff
notes.contentHtml:
The Rausser College of Natural Resources (RCNR) admits students directly into one of its nine undergraduate majors as selected by the applicant at the time of application. Because Rausser College does not admit transfer students as undeclared, each individual major has a distinct set of transfer courses required as major preparation before the applicant is minimally eligible for admission.  


Resources for RCNR transfer applicants: 

  • UC Berkeley Office of Undergraduate Admissions Minimum Requirements for Transfer Admission
  • Rausser College of Natural Resources Transfer Applicants page - includes transfer admission guidelines specific to each Rausser College major, as well as additional information on how to submit a competitive application for your intended major 
  • Rausser College Major Requirements and Sample Degree Plans
  • Rausser College Advising Staff

notes.content: ABOUT THE ECOSYSTEM MANAGEMENT & FORESTRY MAJOR Ecosystem Management & Forestry focuses on the conservation and restoration of the earth's natural resources through hands-on study of the ecology, stewardship, and management of forest, woodland, and grassland ecosystems. The program offers an accredited specialization in Forestry and Natural Resource Management. For additional information, please contact the Ecosystem Management & Forestry advisor at crs.emf.ugrad@berkeley.edu .
notes.contentHtml:
ABOUT THE ECOSYSTEM MANAGEMENT & FORESTRY MAJOR


Ecosystem Management & Forestry focuses on the conservation and restoration of the earth's natural resources through hands-on study of the ecology, stewardship, and management of forest, woodland, and grassland ecosystems. The program offers an accredited specialization in Forestry and Natural Resource Management.


For additional information, please contact the Ecosystem Management & Forestry advisor at crs.emf.ugrad@berkeley.edu.

notes.content: Please Note: Applicants are expected to complete all required coursework by the end of the spring term prior to admission. Majors may allow one course pending in the summer term prior to admission if the major has more than five required courses and at least five courses of the required coursework have been completed; in this case, if a requirement includes both a lecture and lab, the lecture and lab together are treated as one course. Majors which require five or fewer required courses to transfer will not allow required coursework to be completed in the summer prior to admission. Please see the Rausser College of Natural Resources Transfer Applicants page for individual requirements by major. Ecosystem Management & Forestry applicants may have the opportunity to complete a single required course in the summer term prior to admission. If a requirement includes both a lecture and lab, the lecture and lab together are treated as one course. To ensure full articulation of transfer coursework: if a series of courses at a community college is required (e.g., Chemistry 101 + 102 + 103 = CHEM 1A/1AL + CHEM 1B at UC Berkeley), all of the courses in the series must be completed, and must (unless otherwise indicated) be completed at the same community college. Partial completion of the series (e.g., two of the three required courses) may result in no credit toward the requirement(s), and splitting a series of courses between different community colleges is not guaranteed to satisfy the requirement. Courses taken out of series will be evaluated by college faculty during the transfer admissions process. If you have questions about the articulation of a series for your intended major, please email crs.emf.ugrad@berkeley.edu . Minimum Admissions Requirements in Ecosystem Management & Forestry: Equivalent of: READING & COMPOSITION A + READING & COMPOSTION B - satisfied by full UC IGETC, or may be satisfied with AP/IB/A-Level exam credit CHEM 1A and CHEM 1AL - may also be satisfied by AP/IB/A-Level exam credit BIOLOGY 1B - may also be satisfied by AP/IB/A-Level exam credit MATH 16A and MATH 16B; or MATH 51 and MATH 52 - may also be satisfied by AP/IB/A-Level exam credit Highly Recommended (but not required) Prior to Transfer ESPM Environmental Science and ESPM Social Science core courses EPS 50, GEOG 1, or GEOG 40 ECON 1 or ECON 2 (micro-economics and macro-economics) Introductory statistics, environmental science, and geographic information systems (GIS) courses
notes.contentHtml:
Please Note: Applicants are expected to complete all required coursework by the end of the spring term prior to admission. Majors may allow one course pending in the summer term prior to admission if the major has more than five required courses and at least five courses of the required coursework have been completed; in this case, if a requirement includes both a lecture and lab, the lecture and lab together are treated as one course. Majors which require five or fewer required courses to transfer will not allow required coursework to be completed in the summer prior to admission. Please see the Rausser College of Natural Resources Transfer Applicants page for individual requirements by major.


Ecosystem Management & Forestry applicants may have the opportunity to complete a single required course in the summer term prior to admission. If a requirement includes both a lecture and lab, the lecture and lab together are treated as one course.



To ensure full articulation of transfer coursework: if a series of courses at a community college is required (e.g., Chemistry 101 + 102 + 103 = CHEM 1A/1AL + CHEM 1B at UC Berkeley), all of the courses in the series must be completed, and must (unless otherwise indicated) be completed at the same community college. Partial completion of the series (e.g., two of the three required courses) may result in no credit toward the requirement(s), and splitting a series of courses between different community colleges is not guaranteed to satisfy the requirement. Courses taken out of series will be evaluated by college faculty during the transfer admissions process. If you have questions about the articulation of a series for your intended major, please email crs.emf.ugrad@berkeley.edu.


Minimum Admissions Requirements in Ecosystem Management & Forestry:


Equivalent of: 

  • READING & COMPOSITION A + READING & COMPOSTION B - satisfied by full UC IGETC, or may be satisfied with AP/IB/A-Level exam credit
  • CHEM 1A and CHEM 1AL - may also be satisfied by AP/IB/A-Level exam credit
  • BIOLOGY 1B - may also be satisfied by AP/IB/A-Level exam credit
  • MATH 16A and MATH 16B; or MATH 51 and MATH 52 - may also be satisfied by AP/IB/A-Level exam credit



Highly Recommended (but not required) Prior to Transfer

  • ESPM Environmental Science and ESPM Social Science core courses
  • EPS 50, GEOG 1, or GEOG 40 
  • ECON 1 or ECON 2 (micro-economics and macro-economics)
  • Introductory statistics, environmental science, and geographic information systems (GIS) courses
